global_context.scene_description: A bold editorial cartoon-illustrated 5-panel comic strip composition, single image with five equal panels arranged in a 5-panel grid (could be 5 panels in a single horizontal row, or a 2-3 split, but cleanest is 5 small panels in one row OR a 3+2 grid). Each panel features the SAME homeowner cartoon character in a different emotional state, with a labeled stage. Editorial cartoon illustration style, NOT photorealistic, NOT flat infographic. PANEL 1 - 'DENIAL' (yellow/light background): homeowner standing proudly next to a wheezing old AC unit emitting steam puffs, hands on hips, smile, speech bubble: 'It still works!'. PANEL 2 - 'ANGER' (red background): homeowner clenching fists, red-faced, staring at a $20,000 quote paper in his hand, speech bubble: 'TWENTY GRAND?!'. PANEL 3 - 'BARGAINING' (orange background): homeowner sitting on couch with a fan pointed at his face, sweating, speech bubble: 'Maybe one more summer...'. PANEL 4 - 'DEPRESSION' (blue-grey background): homeowner slumped on couch holding an electric bill, depressed posture, speech bubble: 'Why is it always something?'. PANEL 5 - 'ACCEPTANCE' (warm green background): homeowner standing relaxed, holding a clean ASH quote document showing '$9,500 / 0% APR / PRICE MATCH', smiling content, speech bubble: 'I should have called sooner.' Above the entire image, a clean banner-style headline reads in bold uppercase: 'THE 5 STAGES OF AC DREAD.' At the bottom, small italic caption: 'Skip to Stage 5. Call ASH. $9,500. 0% APR. Price match.' Each panel has a thin black border separating it from the next. Mood: self-aware comedy that lands the angle through humor. Editorial cartoon illustration with theatrical color blocking per panel.

lighting.quality: each panel has its own emotional color tone

composition.framing: 5-panel comic strip layout, all panels equal size, headline banner across top, caption at bottom
composition.focal_point: the contrast between the four anxious/dread panels and the relaxed final acceptance panel with the ASH quote
